objective: Complete post-access investigation of kobold.htb as ben. Map the FULL local attack surface for privilege escalation — every service, every interesting file, every path. Do NOT rabbit-hole on any single finding. Enumerate everything first, THEN rank paths. Special focus areas: (1) Arcane Docker Management on port 3552 — how does its auth work, can we access it, what does its database look like, (2) alice user — find credentials or lateral path, she has docker group, (3) PrivateBin container and data volume interactions, (4) any local services, configs, or files that reveal credentials.
in_scope: All files readable by ben or operator group
Arcane Docker Management API on port 3552
PrivateBin container investigation via data volume
User enumeration — alice home dir, credentials, SSH keys
Process memory, environment variables, open file descriptors
allowed_actions: Read files accessible to ben/operator group
Query local HTTP services (curl to localhost)
Run standard enumeration commands (id, ps, ss, find, etc.)
Download and search JS bundles from Arcane frontend
Web search for CVEs and version-specific vulnerabilities
disallowed_actions: Modify files on the target (except in /tmp)
completion_criteria: All localhost services identified and probed
return_conditions: Investigation complete — all completion criteria met
Blocked — cannot proceed without operator guidance

known_facts.docker_socket: root:docker — ben cannot access, alice can
known_facts.arcane_version: v1.13.0 (Go binary, runs as root from /root/arcane_linux_amd64)
known_facts.privatebin_paste_dirs: /privatebin-data/data/12/ and /4e/ — nobody:82 mode 700, ben cannot read
known_facts.cve_2026_23944: Auth bypass for remote environments — no remote envs configured, bypass useless
known_facts.cve_2026_23520: RCE via lifecycle labels — fixed in v1.13.0, target is patched

oracle_already_tried: JWT forgery with default secret 'default-jwt-secret-change-me' — FAILED (401)
JWT forgery with ENCRYPTION_KEY as secret — FAILED
Arcane default creds (admin/admin, admin/password, etc.) — FAILED
CVE-2026-23944 auth bypass — only works for remote envs, none configured
PrivateBin webshell in salt.php via GET param — does not fire (file_get_contents not include)
Direct access to PHP files in PrivateBin data dir — 'File not found'
investigation_priorities: 1. Find alice credentials — she is the path to docker group and root
2. Crack Arcane auth — JWT secret may differ in v1.13.0 binary vs source
3. Read PrivateBin paste content in /12/ and /4e/ — may contain secrets
5. Check for ANY credential material anywhere on the filesystem

notes: Oracle did partial enumeration inline but was rabbit-holing on Arcane JWT forgery. NOIRE should do a DISCIPLINED full-surface sweep, document everything, then rank. The full enumeration output from Oracle's sweep is saved at /tmp/kobold_enum.txt on the attacker box for reference — but NOIRE should confirm everything fresh from the target.
